extraction.one_line_summary: JACOBSEN Fabio won a chaotic sprint after a late crash disrupted leadout trains, beating KITTEL and CALEB EWAN in a photo finish
tactical_patterns: Late-race crash disrupted organized leadouts, allowing Quick-Step without visible train to capitalize on chaos over structured teams
Dimension Data and Lotto-Soudal pushed front positioning aggressively but crash neutralized their leadout investment completely
Max Walscheid lost Trek leadout early, exposing vulnerability of single-rider German leadout chains in technical finishes

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: neutral: fought back to photo finish despite losing leadout, showing resilience but no positioning advantage
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: boxed out mid-sprint despite surviving crash, LottoNL positional work failed to translate
rider_signals.CALEB EWAN: positive: threatened win with unconventional low sprint position despite no visible Mitchelton leadout present
rider_signals.JACOBSEN Fabio: positive: capitalized on chaos without traditional leadout, revealing tactical opportunism over planned execution
